rationale.R1.2: The artifact makes a causal argument (prompt verbosity × noise level → WER variation → named-entity substitution → reproducibility bounds → constrained decoding recommendation); logical soundness of this chain is a natural target of any 'check'.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims are asserted (WER 3.1%–8.7% across 12 conditions, named-entity substitution as majority source of error) with no visible supporting tables or figures in the artifact; verifying that stated numbers are coherent with the described experimental design is load-bearing under a content check.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation 'Check this.' is equally consistent with a proofreading or copy-editing intent; writing quality is therefore a plausible load-bearing dimension that cannot be ruled out.

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want a substantive review—checking logical consistency and whether the empirical claims (WER figures, error-distribution findings) are adequately supported by the described experimental design—rather than a writing/language review focused on grammar, clarity, and prose quality?
